notes: ROOT CAUSE (reproduced 2026-06-09): The test sends sha256('global:initialize_user_stats')[:8] = [254,243,72,98,251,130,168,213] as the Anchor instruction discriminator. The deployed Drift binary (dRiftyHA39MWEi3m9aunc5MzRF1JYuBsbn6VPcn33UH, sha256=72cc3062...) does NOT recognise these bytes: it returns AnchorError InstructionFallbackNotFound (Custom 101) for every instruction tried. CONFIRMED NOT A FORK-VALIDATOR ARTIFACT: (1) the binary is byte-identical to the live mainnet deployment; (2) identical InstructionFallbackNotFound failures for the same discriminator bytes were observed in real confirmed mainnet transactions (three transactions from fee-payer Go4w6HVs7nxAjAckPUCibTecXXzs4LEqC3zoSsKRxx1z on 2026-06-07, all returning {InstructionError:[0,{Custom:101}]}); (3) exhaustive binary search of the 6.7 MB ELF finds no sha256-based discriminator bytes in any section (.text, .rodata, .data.rel.ro) or as BPF lddw split-immediate halves. WHAT IS NEEDED TO FIX: determine the actual instruction bytes the deployed binary accepts — either by fetching raw instruction data from a confirmed-successful mainnet Drift transaction (requires a public RPC that exposes CPI inner-instruction data), or by using DriftClient with a real funded account and observing the encoded bytes. The adapter hardcoded discriminators (lib.rs lines 21-24) and the camelizeDriftIdl helper in drift-insurance-fund-flow.ts will both need updating once the correct values are known.
